stat_of_week: 52 - Sugar Land Skeeters scored 52 runs in a single week, the most explosive offensive output of the season and a number that single-handedly decided multiple categories against North Correa
thriller_note: A dead-even tie where General Hospital's elite pitching (Wrobleski's 0.69 ERA, Fried's 0.63 WHIP) was perfectly countered by Hiroshima's league-leading 12 NSVH and McLean's 17-strikeout showcase, leaving both teams frustrated and no closer to separation in the standings.
key_storyline: Sugar Land Skeeters put on the most dominant offensive performance of the season, posting 52 runs and 15 home runs to obliterate North Correa 5-4 despite North Correa's own excellent pitching week featuring Glasnow (0.25 WHIP) and Sale (0.50 WHIP). The Skeeters' lineup went scorched-earth with Max Muncy (.583 OBP, 3 HR), Drake Baldwin (8 R), and Matt Olson (3 HR, 9 RBI) all going nuclear simultaneously, confirming this offense as the most dangerous in the league when it gets hot. That Sugar Land still only won 5-4 despite those historic run totals underscores how elite North Correa's pitching is — and sets up a genuinely frightening Skeeters roster that is peaking at the right time.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers carry the league's highest luck delta at +1.33 and posted just 3 HR and a 5.28 ERA this week, winning 6 categories against Buttville largely on the strength of 11 stolen bases and opponent underperformance rather than any dominant statistical profile.
unlucky_reason: Wonder Wharf carries the season's worst luck delta at -0.62, and this week's tie against Canberra — where they posted better counting stats in runs, hits, and RBI while also throwing more innings with a lower ERA — continues a brutal pattern of strong performance failing to convert into wins.
records_broken: The Albuquerque Bombers tied the season record for team strikeouts in a week with 77 K, matching the mark set by the Abbotsford-Colby Twins in Week 2, driven by Kyle Harrison (15 K) and Landen Roupp (13 K).
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 52 runs and 15 HR in a single week is a statement; this offense is the most dangerous in the league and the Skeeters remain the most consistent team across all five weeks | up

2. General Hospital | A 5-5 tie stings when you expected a win, but Wrobleski's 0.69 ERA, Fried's 0.63 WHIP, and 5 QS confirm this rotation is elite; Kumar Rocker waiting in the wings makes this even scarier | same

3. BaseballPro77 | Lost 5-3 but Murakami (3 HR), Trout (2 HR, 2 SB), and Basallo (.611 OBP) all showed up — this offense is loaded and Strahm adds late-inning reliability to a staff that needed it | same

4. Athletic Park Dragons | Led the league with 13 HR and 39 RBI and still lost 6-4 because the pitching staff posted a 6.31 ERA with zero quality starts — the talent ceiling is enormous but the rotation is a genuine crisis | down

5. Albuquerque Bombers | An 8-1 demolition fueled by Harrison, Roupp, and a fully activated lineup; four waiver adds including Josh Jung show a manager aggressively patching holes, and this week proved the roster has real teeth | up

6. North Correa | Lost a heartbreaker 5-4 despite Glasnow posting a 0.25 WHIP and Sale a 0.50 WHIP — you simply cannot pitch better than that and lose; Ohtani's 3 SB and the 10-steal week show elite multi-category upside | same

7. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean's 17 K and 12 NSVH as a team are real weapons; the 5-5 tie feels like a loss given the opportunity, but the saves-and-holds structure makes this team uniquely dangerous in a close race | up

8. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | A 5-3 win with Aranda (3 HR), Chisholm, and Griffin delivering is encouraging; the -0.43 luck delta continues to haunt but this roster is too talented to stay in the middle tier long | same

9. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6-4 over a loaded Dragons squad — Kurtz (8 R, 3 HR), Suzuki (3 HR, .524 OBP), and 5 QS show a team that is quietly building something real; Ragans and Misiorowski both looked sharp | up

10. Canberra Wombats | Cal Raleigh (4 HR, 6 R) and Christian Walker (3 HR) gave the offense a pulse, but Aaron Nola's 11.00 ERA is a disaster and a team ERA of 5.59 with just 2 QS won't sustain ties, let alone wins | same

11. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | The worst luck delta in the league (-0.62) is becoming an identity crisis; Harris (9 RBI), Moniak (2 HR), and solid pitching all week and they still can't get past a tie — Dustin May adds rotation hope | down

12. Buttville Skidmarks | De La Cruz and Caminero each went 3 HR and the team still only scored 24 runs; 5 NSVH is a real asset but Bradish's 2.03 WHIP is alarming and the offense is too inconsistent to trust | same

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got blasted 8-1 in every pitching category and the rotation gave up a 5.81 ERA; dropping McGreevy without a meaningful replacement signals a front office that doesn't have answers yet | down

14. Benchwarmers | The +1.33 luck delta is the highest in the league and the most unsustainable number in the data — 3 HR, a 5.28 ERA, and a thin power profile mean the house of cards is one bad week from collapsing | same
waiver_highlights: Albuquerque Bombers | A | Added Josh Jung (3B) — contributed immediately with 1 HR, 3 RBI, 3 R, and .375 OBP in his debut week; exactly the kind of high-floor add a contender needs mid-season
Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Braxton Garrett (SP) and Keaton Winn (SP,RP) — rotation depth behind Harrison and Roupp was a glaring need, and adding two arms in one week addresses the vulnerability

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | B | Added Dustin May (SP) and Ildemaro Vargas (1B,2B) — May gives the rotation a genuine upside arm returning from injury, and Vargas contributed immediately with 4 R, 1 HR, 4 RBI this week
General Hospital | B | Added Kumar Rocker (SP); dropped three underperformers — consolidating roster spots into one high-upside arm is savvy management when your rotation is already the league's best

Athletic Park Dragons | C | Added Dalton Rushing (C); dropped Colton Cowser (OF) — Rushing has upside behind the plate but dropping Cowser's speed and OBP potential for a catching upgrade of uncertain timeline is a debatable trade-off

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Bachman and Vodnik (RP); dropped Mark Vientos — sacrificing a power bat for two bullpen arms makes sense given their 5 QS week, but losing Vientos' offensive upside is a real cost
